label: Google Analytics 4
description: Query web and app analytics data from Google Analytics 4 properties including traffic, events, conversions, and audience metrics.

form.title: Connect Google Analytics 4
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to read analytics data from your GA4 properties.

methods.label: In-Browser Connect
methods.description: Opens Google sign-in in your browser. No credentials required — just click Connect.

methods.label: Sign in with Google (OAuth 2.0)
methods.description: Opens a browser consent screen so Anton can read your GA4 properties. Requires setting up a Google Cloud OAuth client — steps below. No passwords stored, revocable from your Google Account at any time.
methods.how_to:
## Sign in with Google (OAuth 2.0 — Bring Your Own Client)

Anton doesn't ship a hosted OAuth client for GA4 yet, so you'll create one in Google Cloud Console first. It takes about 5 minutes.

## 1 — Enable the GA4 API

1. Go to [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and pick or create a project.
2. Navigate to **APIs & Services → Library**.
3. Search for **Google Analytics Data API** and click **Enable**.

## 2 — Configure the OAuth consent screen

1. Go to **APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen**.
2. Choose **External** (or **Internal** if you're on a Workspace org) and click **Create**.
3. Fill in the app name (e.g. `Anton`) and your email. Save and continue through all steps.
4. Under **Test users**, add the Google account that owns your GA4 property.

## 3 — Create a Desktop OAuth client

1. Go to **APIs & Services → Credentials → Create credentials → OAuth client ID**.
2. Select **Desktop app** as the application type. Name it `Anton`.
3. Click **Create**. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret** shown in the dialog.
4. Paste both into the fields below and click **Submit**.

## 4 — Authorize

Anton opens your browser to Google's consent screen. Sign in with the account that has access to your GA4 properties, review the requested scopes, and click **Continue**.

## Notes

- The `analytics.readonly` scope grants read-only access — Anton cannot modify any data.
- If the consent screen shows a warning about an unverified app, click **Advanced → Go to (app name)** — this is expected for apps in testing mode.
- To revoke access later, visit [Google Account → Third-party apps](https://myaccount.google.com/permissions) and remove the entry.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth client ID
fields.description: From Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials. Use a Desktop app OAuth client.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth client secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID above; copied from the same row in Credentials.

methods.label: Service account
methods.description: Use a Google service account JSON key to access GA4 without user interaction. Ideal for server-side or automated workflows where no human login is needed.
methods.how_to:
## Service account access for GA4

A service account lets Anton query GA4 without a user's OAuth session — good for automated or headless setups.

## 1 — Enable the GA4 API

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and select your project.
2. Go to **APIs & Services → Library**, search for **Google Analytics Data API**, and enable it.

## 2 — Create a service account

1. Go to **IAM & Admin → Service Accounts → Create service account**.
2. Name it something like `anton-ga4`. Click **Create and continue** through the role steps (no special IAM role needed here — access is granted in GA4 itself).
3. Open the service account, switch to the **Keys** tab, click **Add key → Create new key → JSON**, and download the file.

## 3 — Grant access in GA4

1. Go to [Google Analytics](https://analytics.google.com/) → **Admin** (gear icon).
2. Select the account and/or property you want Anton to access.
3. Click **Account Access Management** (or **Property Access Management** for property-level access).
4. Click **+** → **Add users**, paste the service account email (e.g. `anton-ga4@your-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com`), and assign at least the **Viewer** role.
5. Click **Add**.

## 4 — Paste the JSON key

Copy the full contents of the downloaded JSON key file and paste it into the **Service account JSON** field below.

## Notes

- The JSON key is sensitive — anyone who has it can read your GA4 data. Rotate it in Cloud Console if you suspect exposure.
- You can grant access to multiple properties by repeating step 3 for each one.

fields.name: service_account_json
fields.label: Service account JSON
fields.description: Paste the entire contents of the JSON key file downloaded from Google Cloud Console.
